content.problemStatement:
You are given a matrix AAA of non-negative integers with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN). For each valid iii and jjj, let's denote the element in the iii-th row and jjj-th column by Ai,jA_{i,j}Ai,j​.

You need to find a matrix BBB with N+1N+1N+1 rows and N+1N+1N+1 columns (numbered similarly) such that:


each element of this matrix is a digit between 000 and 999 (inclusive)
Ai,j=Bi,j+Bi+1,j+Bi,j+1+Bi+1,j+1A_{i,j} = B_{i,j}+B_{i+1,j}+B_{i,j+1}+B_{i+1,j+1}Ai,j​=Bi,j​+Bi+1,j​+Bi,j+1​+Bi+1,j+1​ for each valid i,ji,ji,j
The matrix AAA is chosen in such a way that at least one solution exists. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one of them.
constraints: for the matrix AAA, at least one valid matrix BBB exists
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains NNN space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,…,Ai,NA_{i,1}, A_{i,2}, \ldots, A_{i,N}Ai,1​,Ai,2​,…,Ai,N​.
content.outputFormat: Print N+1N+1N+1 lines. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines should contain N+1N+1N+1 characters Bi,1,Bi,2,…,Bi,N+1B_{i,1}, B_{i,2}, \ldots, B_{i,N+1}Bi,1​,Bi,2​,…,Bi,N+1​.
